nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore pattern file for the Understand-Anything analysis tool, specifying directories and file patterns (such as REFERENCE-REPO/, bucket/, and transcript text files) to exclude from knowledge graph generation.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Minimal Understand-Anything plugin configuration setting the output language to English for all generated knowledge graph textual content.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer that marks the backend/db directory as a Python package, enabling imports from the db module.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package initializer that marks the backend/rag directory as a Python package, enabling imports from the rag module.

nodes.name: chunker.py
nodes.summary: Deprecated stub file previously implementing an LLM-based semantic chunker for ingestion; replaced by the character-based sliding-window splitter in ingestion.py and kept only to prevent stale import errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Kept as an empty stub to avoid ModuleNotFoundError from cached .pyc files; safe to delete once __pycache__ is cleared.

nodes.name: document_parser.py
nodes.summary: Deprecated stub file previously implementing a Docling-based PDF/DOCX parser; replaced by PyMuPDF-based text extraction in ingestion.py and kept only to prevent stale import errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Kept as an empty stub to avoid ModuleNotFoundError from cached .pyc files; safe to delete once __pycache__ is cleared.

nodes.name: enricher.py
nodes.summary: Deprecated stub file previously implementing per-chunk LLM contextual enrichment via Ollama; replaced by a lightweight inline header in ingestion.py and kept only to prevent stale import errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Kept as an empty stub to avoid ModuleNotFoundError from cached .pyc files; safe to delete once __pycache__ is cleared.

nodes.name: structured_extractor.py
nodes.summary: Deprecated stub file previously implementing per-chunk LLM extraction of patient/provider/billing data into SQLite; replaced by direct columnar SQL ingestion in ingestion.py and kept only to prevent stale import errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Kept as an empty stub to avoid ModuleNotFoundError from cached .pyc files; safe to delete once __pycache__ is cleared.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Backend Python dependency manifest listing FastAPI, ChromaDB, sentence-transformers, PyMuPDF, psycopg2, sqlglot, rank-bm25, and other packages required for the RAG pipeline, structured SQL retrieval, and API server.

nodes.name: migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py
nodes.summary: Command-line database migration script that reads all tables from a SQLite database and bulk-inserts them into PostgreSQL using batch fetching, column padding, and sequence reset; supports dry-run mode to preview the migration without committing.

nodes.name: migrate
nodes.summary: Core migration function that opens both SQLite and PostgreSQL connections, iterates all source tables, batch-copies rows with column padding, commits, and resets PostgreSQL sequences; accepts a dry_run flag to skip commits.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses CLI arguments for SQLite path, PostgreSQL DSN, and dry-run flag, invokes migrate(), and prints a per-table row count summary.

nodes.name: purge_structured_chroma.py
nodes.summary: Maintenance script for selectively removing tabular/structured documents from a ChromaDB collection by metadata filter, with optional collection rebuild and dry-run preview mode.

nodes.name: purge_tabular_docs
nodes.summary: Connects to ChromaDB over HTTP, retrieves documents matching a tabular source_type filter, optionally deletes them in batches, and can drop and recreate the entire collection when rebuild is requested.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses CLI arguments for ChromaDB host, port, collection name, dry-run, and rebuild flags, then invokes purge_tabular_docs().

nodes.name: alice_johnson_bill_2025-10-27.json
nodes.summary: Sample patient billing record for Alice Johnson containing itemized charges, insurance coverage details, payment status, and provider NPI — used as test data for the medical RAG ingestion pipeline.

nodes.name: john_smith_record_2025-09-15.json
nodes.summary: Sample patient medical record for John Smith containing visit type, chief complaint, diagnosis, treatment plan, vitals, allergies, and provider information — used as test data for the medical RAG ingestion pipeline.

nodes.name: dr_robert_chen.json
nodes.summary: Sample provider profile for Dr. Robert Chen containing NPI, specialty, license details, hospital affiliations, and contact information — used as test data for the medical RAG provider lookup pipeline.

nodes.name: dr_sarah_williams.json
nodes.summary: Sample provider profile for Dr. Sarah Williams containing NPI, specialty, license details, hospital affiliations, and contact information — used as test data for the medical RAG provider lookup pipeline.

nodes.name: sources.yaml
nodes.summary: Stream ingestion configuration file that maps CSV/Excel column signatures to canonical field names, data types, PHI denylist tokens, and query exposure flags used by the catalog_admin ingestion pipeline.

nodes.name: sql_schema_metadata.yaml
nodes.summary: Few-shot example library providing question-to-SQL pairs for the LLM text-to-SQL generation stage, teaching safe cast patterns, NULL guards, date formatting, and join idioms specific to the medical RAG PostgreSQL schema.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: Streamlit frontend application providing a chat interface for the medical RAG system, enabling users to query patient records, billing data, and provider information with inline source citation and relevance scoring display.

nodes.name: api
nodes.summary: Generic HTTP helper that dispatches requests to the backend API, raises status errors, returns parsed JSON, and displays Streamlit error messages on connection or HTTP failures.

nodes.name: render_sources
nodes.summary: Renders a collapsible Streamlit expander showing retrieved source documents with metadata tags (patient, provider, record type, date), relevance score labels, and optional raw chunk text display.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Frontend Python dependency manifest listing Streamlit, requests, and pandas — the minimal packages required for the RAG chat UI.

nodes.name: migrate_sqlite_to_postgres.py
nodes.summary: Top-level copy of the database migration script that reads all tables from SQLite and bulk-inserts them into PostgreSQL with batch fetching, column padding, and sequence reset; supports dry-run mode.

nodes.name: migrate
nodes.summary: Core migration function that opens both SQLite and PostgreSQL connections, iterates all source tables, batch-copies rows with column padding, commits, and resets PostgreSQL sequences; accepts a dry_run flag to skip commits.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses CLI arguments for SQLite path, PostgreSQL DSN, and dry-run flag, invokes migrate(), and prints a per-table row count summary.

nodes.name: purge_structured_chroma.py
nodes.summary: Top-level copy of the ChromaDB maintenance script for selectively removing tabular/structured documents by metadata filter, with optional collection rebuild and dry-run preview mode.

nodes.name: purge_tabular_docs
nodes.summary: Connects to ChromaDB over HTTP, retrieves documents matching a tabular source_type filter, optionally deletes them in batches, and can drop and recreate the entire collection when rebuild is requested.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses CLI arguments for ChromaDB host, port, collection name, dry-run, and rebuild flags, then invokes purge_tabular_docs().
